tab1.role_description: A junior Data Analyst at an Indian e-commerce, fintech, or consumer internet company spends their week writing SQL queries to pull data from warehouse platforms like Snowflake or Redshift, building Power BI or Tableau dashboards for business teams, and preparing weekly performance reports for product and marketing stakeholders. Daily tasks include cleaning raw data in Python (Pandas), conducting cohort analyses, tracking KPIs like DAU, retention rates, and conversion funnels, and responding to ad hoc data requests from business leads. They coordinate with data engineers on pipeline issues, validate data quality, and present findings in weekly business review meetings — often to non-technical audiences who need clear, jargon-free narratives.
tab1.narrative_para1: A junior Data Analyst at an Indian e-commerce, fintech, or consumer internet company spends their week writing SQL queries to pull data from warehouse platforms like Snowflake or Redshift, building Power BI or Tableau dashboards for business teams, and preparing weekly performance reports for product and marketing stakeholders. Daily tasks include cleaning raw data in Python (Pandas), conducting cohort analyses, tracking KPIs like DAU, retention rates, and conversion funnels, and responding to ad hoc data requests from business leads. They coordinate with data engineers on pipeline issues, validate data quality, and present findings in weekly business review meetings — often to non-technical audiences who need clear, jargon-free narratives.
tab1.narrative_para2: With 60% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. ChatGPT and Power BI Copilot automate SQL query generation, data cleaning scripts, and dashboard commentary, covering a large share of routine analyst output. Copilot in Excel accelerates pivot table analysis, formula writing, and data exploration. AI tools assist in anomaly detection and generating first-draft analytical reports. However, data analysts frame the right business questions, contextualise data anomalies against operational events, and translate insights into actionable recommendations that AI cannot generate without business context. They build relationships with business stakeholders to understand decision needs and communicate findings persuasively to non-technical audiences. Ethical handling of sensitive customer data and bias identification in analyses requires human judgement. The tools currently reshaping this role include ChatGPT, Power BI Copilot, and Copilot in Excel (Microsoft 365 Copilot) — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
tab1.narrative_para3: B.Tech, BCA, B.Sc (Statistics/Mathematics), MBA, or M.Sc graduates are well suited. Those who enjoy finding stories in numbers, are curious about business outcomes, and can communicate clearly with non-technical stakeholders will thrive. Starting at Rs.3-5 lakh, salaries grow to Rs.6-11 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.10-18 lakh at senior analyst level, and Rs.18-35 lakh for analytics managers. SQL and Python proficiency combined with strong data visualisation skills (Power BI or Tableau) are the core hiring requirements. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG, MBA.
tab1.typical_employers_india: MNCs, IT Product Companies, Startups, IT Services
tab1.common_entry_paths: Campus Placements, MBA Internships, Lateral Hiring

tab1.role_description: A junior Software Developer at an Indian tech company or product startup spends their week writing and reviewing code in Python, Java, or JavaScript, fixing bugs raised on Jira, and attending daily standups in an Agile sprint. Daily tasks include building REST APIs, writing unit tests, peer reviewing pull requests on GitHub, and deploying code through Jenkins or GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines. They work within version control workflows, follow coding standards, and coordinate with QA engineers before releases. In Indian service companies, they may work on client-facing modules requiring VAPT compliance; in product startups, they work directly on the core product codebase with faster iteration cycles.
tab1.narrative_para1: A junior Software Developer at an Indian tech company or product startup spends their week writing and reviewing code in Python, Java, or JavaScript, fixing bugs raised on Jira, and attending daily standups in an Agile sprint. Daily tasks include building REST APIs, writing unit tests, peer reviewing pull requests on GitHub, and deploying code through Jenkins or GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines. They work within version control workflows, follow coding standards, and coordinate with QA engineers before releases. In Indian service companies, they may work on client-facing modules requiring VAPT compliance; in product startups, they work directly on the core product codebase with faster iteration cycles.
tab1.narrative_para2: With 55% AI exposure, this role is actively AI-Evolving. GitHub Copilot and Tabnine assist with code completion, boilerplate generation, and unit test scaffolding, covering a significant portion of routine coding tasks. ChatGPT and Claude are used for debugging assistance, code explanation, and generating first-draft implementations. AI accelerates repetitive development tasks but requires human review for correctness, security, and architectural fit. However, software developers make architectural decisions, define abstractions, and design systems that meet complex, evolving business requirements. They debug intricate production issues involving concurrency, distributed systems, or data corruption where AI lacks sufficient context. Code review, mentoring junior developers, and aligning technical solutions to product goals demand human communication and accountability. The tools currently reshaping this role include GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Tabnine — each accelerating routine work while leaving the most complex, context-dependent decisions firmly in human hands.
tab1.narrative_para3: B.Tech, B.Sc (CS), MCA, or M.Tech graduates are the core fit. Those with strong problem-solving instincts, comfort with reading documentation, and the ability to break complex requirements into working code will thrive. Starting salaries of Rs.3.5-6 lakh grow to Rs.7-12 lakh at 2-3 years, Rs.12-20 lakh at senior developer level, and Rs.20-40 lakh for tech leads and architects. DSA (Data Structures and Algorithms) proficiency is the most critical hiring filter. Open source contributions and strong GitHub profiles accelerate progression. Eligible programme levels: UG, PG.
tab1.typical_employers_india: MNCs, IT Product Companies, Startups, IT Services
